2605.05055.title: Adaptive Learning Strategies for AoA-Based Outdoor Localization: A Comprehensive Framework
2605.05055.paper_summary: Localization in 5G and 6G networks is essential for important use cases such as intelligent transportation, smart factories, and smart cities. Although deep learning has enabled improving localization accuracy, depending on the deployment scenario and the effort required for dataset collection campaigns on a given infrastructure, the training process for localization models can vary significantly. Furthermore, with respect to feature selection, recent works have demonstrated the robustness of angle-of-arrival (AoA) based localization. In view of these two points, we propose an adaptive framework for AoA-based localization that consists of two alternative learning strategies, each suited either for large or small training datasets. The proposed framework is evaluated on a real, massive multiple input multiple output (mMIMO) orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) outdoor channel state information (CSI) dataset. First, we investigate offline learning when large training datasets are available; we propose a hierarchical framework that first distinguishes between line of sight (LoS) and non line of sight (NLoS) regions and then moves to more fine grained localization in the respective region. This approach provides high-performance localization through accumulated batch retraining and an integrated hyperparameter optimization mechanism. Second, when only a small training dataset is available, an online learning framework is proposed, using incremental tree-based and ensemble-based models for handling streaming data and continuously updating mode, as well as an online few-shot learning model for rapidly initializing new classes from a limited labeled support set. These results showcase that highly accurate robust localization can be achieved incrementally during network operation by exploiting online learning, alleviating the need for large dataset collection campaigns.
2605.05055.paper_summary_zh: 5G 和 6G 網絡中的定位對於智能交通、智能工廠和智慧城市等重要應用場景至關重要。雖然深度學習已經提高了定位的準確性，但根據部署場景和在特定基礎設施上進行數據集收集活動所需的努力，定位模型的訓練過程可能會有顯著差異。此外，關於特徵選擇，最近的研究已經證明了基於到達角（AoA）定位的穩健性。基於這兩點，我們提出了一個基於 AoA 的自適應框架，該框架由兩種替代學習策略組成，分別適用於大型或小型訓練數據集。所提出的框架在一個真實的、大規模的多輸入多輸出（mMIMO）正交頻分複用（OFDM）戶外通道狀態信息（CSI）數據集上進行了評估。首先，當大型訓練數據集可用時，我們研究離線學習；我們提出了一個分層框架，首先區分視距（LoS）和非視距（NLoS）區域，然後在各自區域內進行更細緻的定位。這種方法通過累積批量再訓練和集成超參數優化機制提供高性能的定位。其次，當只有小型訓練數據集可用時，提出了一個在線學習框架，使用增量樹基和集成基模型來處理流數據並持續更新模式，以及一個在線少樣本學習模型，快速從有限的標記支持集初始化新類別。這些結果展示了在網絡運行過程中通過利用在線學習可以逐步實現高準確度的穩健定位，減輕了對大型數據集收集活動的需求。
2605.05055.author: Bac Trinh-Nguyen et.al.
2605.05055.authors: Bac Trinh-Nguyen, Sara Berri, Sin G. Teo, Tram Truong-Huu, Arsenia Chorti
